frameworks.name: signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: response criterion shifts under degraded vision as alternative account for blindsight

frameworks.name: perceptual-vs-decisional dissociation
frameworks.one_line: awareness can be impaired relative to visual sensitivity, not merely a criterion artifact

core_question: Can signal detection theory's criterion-artifact account fully explain blindsight phenomena?
key_assumptions: blindsight involves residual visual sensitivity without commensurate awareness
criterion effects and perceptual effects are in principle separable
key_findings: not all criterion effects in blindsight are decidedly nonperceptual
awareness is not completely abolished for some stimuli in some patients
in other cases awareness is clearly impaired relative to visual sensitivity
interpretations: criterion-artifact account does not jointly explain the key blindsight findings
the relative dissociation between sensitivity and awareness is what makes blindsight important
